About.title: A public content layer that helps teams align before they commit to build.
About.summary: The hub connects scenario framing, architecture direction, editorial insight, and community participation so teams can move from curiosity to execution with less ambiguity.

scenarioLedDelivery.title: Scenario-led delivery
scenarioLedDelivery.description: We shape assets around business journeys, operating risks, and measurable outcome moves rather than isolated demos.

reusableArchitecture.title: Reusable architecture
reusableArchitecture.description: Reference patterns accelerate design decisions across orchestration, knowledge, trust, and edge execution.

workingCommunity.title: Working community
workingCommunity.description: Operators, architects, and product teams share methods, proof points, and delivery patterns across sectors.

howUsedItems: Stakeholders browse scenarios to align on business value and workflow change.
Architecture teams use reference patterns to reduce design churn before delivery begins.
Program leaders share insight articles to align controls, ownership, and rollout expectations.
Working groups and labs create a repeatable feedback loop across teams and sectors.
About.startFromSection: Start from the section that fits your need

startWithScenarios.label: Start with scenarios
startWithScenarios.description: Use business journeys when the problem statement is still forming.

startWithArchitecture.label: Start with architecture
startWithArchitecture.description: Use reference views when the delivery model and controls need alignment.

startWithCommunity.label: Start with community
startWithCommunity.description: Use programs and labs when teams need peer exchange and practical review.

goToAssets.label: Go to assets
goToAssets.description: Use the asset library when the team is ready for implementation-oriented exploration.

Scenarios.title: Explore AI delivery scenarios by business journey
Scenarios.summary: Each scenario connects business outcomes, phases, metrics, and reusable reference architectures.

intelligentCustomerOperations.title: Intelligent customer operations
intelligentCustomerOperations.summary: Reshape service operations around grounded copilots, policy-aware automation, and continuous quality loops.
intelligentCustomerOperations.businessOutcome: Cut handle times while raising first-contact resolution across digital and voice channels.

phases.title: Observe and prioritize
phases.description: Map high-frequency intents, exception paths, policy dependencies, and current drop-off points across channels.

phases.title: Ground and automate
phases.description: Introduce retrieval-augmented copilots, guided resolution plays, and event-driven next actions.

phases.title: Measure and refine
phases.description: Track intake quality, escalation handling, and sentiment recovery through a weekly improvement loop.

regulatedRiskCaseOrchestration.title: Regulated risk case orchestration
regulatedRiskCaseOrchestration.summary: Coordinate document review, analyst workbenches, and human approval for high-trust financial workflows.
regulatedRiskCaseOrchestration.businessOutcome: Shorten risk review cycles without weakening traceability, approval, and policy control.

phases.title: Standardize evidence intake
phases.description: Normalize documents, transaction records, and review notes into a consistent case timeline.

phases.title: Prepare assisted decisions
phases.description: Surface anomalies, pre-fill rationale, and keep recommendations aligned with internal control language.

phases.title: Close the loop with lineage
phases.description: Persist every decision, exception, and supporting artifact for downstream audit and model governance.

fieldServiceKnowledgeAcceleration.title: Field service knowledge acceleration
fieldServiceKnowledgeAcceleration.summary: Give technicians multimodal guidance, parts intelligence, and completion capture inside the workflow.
fieldServiceKnowledgeAcceleration.businessOutcome: Raise first-time fix rates and compress ramp time for newly trained field teams.

phases.title: Digitize tacit knowledge
phases.description: Capture expert procedures, parts dependencies, and exception handling as guided digital plays.

phases.title: Assist in the field
phases.description: Blend asset history, troubleshooting trees, and visual recognition into one field experience.

phases.title: Feed the loop
phases.description: Turn job outcomes and technician feedback into updated content and better dispatch logic.

Architectures.title: Reference architectures for trusted AI delivery
Architectures.summary: Connect experience, orchestration, knowledge, control, and execution layers with reusable patterns.

agenticServiceMesh.title: Agentic service mesh
agenticServiceMesh.summary: A modular orchestration pattern for copilots and automation that decouples policy, memory, routing, and execution.

layers.title: Experience layer
layers.description: Desktops, CRMs, portals, and channel integrations surface task context, recommendations, and approvals.

layers.title: Agent orchestration layer
layers.description: Intent routing, prompt composition, tool calls, fallback logic, and supervisor control live here.

layers.title: Knowledge and memory layer
layers.description: Document retrieval, semantic memory, session state, and policy references are versioned and governed.

layers.title: Execution layer
layers.description: Workflow engines and enterprise systems perform updates, case actions, notifications, and data writes.

governance: Separate prompt assets from policy assets so each can be reviewed independently.
Log every tool call, approval handoff, and generated rationale.
Keep rollback controls for model and retrieval changes.
deploymentNotes: Start with a narrow domain and a single channel before widening tool access.
Use a shared trace identifier between orchestration and downstream systems.
Treat confidence thresholds as a business control, not just a model setting.

knowledgeGovernanceFabric.title: Knowledge governance fabric
knowledgeGovernanceFabric.summary: A governed content pipeline for high-trust retrieval that emphasizes freshness, lineage, and approval.

layers.title: Source intake
layers.description: Policies, documents, tickets, and expert notes are normalized, classified, and versioned on the way in.

layers.title: Curation layer
layers.description: Editorial workflows flag conflicts, missing metadata, and stale assets before they reach retrieval.

layers.title: Serving layer
layers.description: Search indexes, vector embeddings, and permission-aware retrieval serve context by role and workflow state.

layers.title: Feedback loop
layers.description: Usage analytics, no-answer reports, and reviewer overrides feed continuous curation and retirement decisions.

governance: Attach source lineage and review status to every retrievable artifact.
Enforce access scope before retrieval and before answer generation.
Measure retrieval quality with human review in sensitive scenarios.
deploymentNotes: Define publishing SLAs for high-volatility policy areas.
Separate draft, approved, and retired knowledge states in storage and in the experience.
Hold the business accountable for content freshness, not just the platform team.

trustControlPlane.title: Trust control plane
trustControlPlane.summary: A horizontal control plane for evaluation, approval, policy enforcement, and operational risk management.

layers.title: Policy definition
layers.description: Business rules, escalation thresholds, and approval paths are declared as reusable controls.

layers.title: Runtime enforcement
layers.description: Requests are checked for access, confidence, risk tier, and mandatory review before an action runs.

layers.title: Evaluation service
layers.description: Offline and online evaluation score recommendation quality, hallucination risk, and drift metrics.

layers.title: Evidence store
layers.description: Decision packages, review outcomes, and audit material are retained for governance and remediation.

governance: Controls advance independently of model or workflow changes.
Make exceptions explicit with an owner, an expiry, and a rationale.
Use a policy simulator before promoting controls to production.
deploymentNotes: Integrate case systems early to retain analyst trust.
Treat evaluation datasets as governance assets and refresh them regularly.
Give risk teams self-serve visibility without depending on engineering changes.

edgeInferenceBackbone.title: Edge inference backbone
edgeInferenceBackbone.summary: A resilient pattern for field and factory settings where local assisted intelligence tolerates connectivity loss.

layers.title: Device experience
layers.description: Tablets, ruggedized devices, and wearables present guided tasks, image capture, and parts lookup.

layers.title: Local inference and cache
layers.description: Compact models and curated knowledge packs support field assistance when bandwidth is constrained.

layers.title: Sync and telemetry
layers.description: Bidirectional sync handles work orders, asset updates, and deferred evidence upload.

layers.title: Central learning loop
layers.description: Headquarters retrains, reviews field outcomes, and ships improved job plays back to the edge.

governance: Scope local models to approved offline tasks only.
Encrypt local caches and auto-expire sensitive content.
Separate operational telemetry from employee performance data.
deploymentNotes: Design graceful degradation before optimizing peak online performance.
Version field job plays so different shifts never see ambiguous instructions.
Treat sync success and content staleness as first-class health metrics.

Insights.title: Short insights that help teams align on operational choices
Insights.summary: Practical perspectives that connect governance, experience design, knowledge systems, and productized delivery.

fromPrototypesToGovernedAiProducts.title: From prototypes to governed AI products
fromPrototypesToGovernedAiProducts.summary: Why many pilots stall, and how portfolio management, ownership, and delivery design must change.
keyPoints: Prototype success rarely predicts production adoption on its own.
Governance must be embedded in the product flow, not bolted on after launch.
Teams need reusable controls, shared telemetry, and clear asset ownership.

bodySections.title: Failure modes
bodySections.description: Many pilots prove a model can produce output without proving the surrounding workflow can absorb that output safely and repeatably.

bodySections.title: What actually scales
bodySections.description: The strongest programs industrialize evaluation, prompt and policy versioning, and business-facing review paths before expanding use cases.

bodySections.title: What to do next
bodySections.description: Treat AI delivery as a product portfolio with explicit service tiers, reusable components, and the operational metrics business owners actually care about.

designingRetrievalForHighTrustWorkflows.title: Designing retrieval for high-trust workflows
designingRetrievalForHighTrustWorkflows.summary: Retrieval quality depends as much on editorial and governance systems as on embeddings and chunking.
keyPoints: The higher the stakes, the more freshness and lineage matter.
Authorization and review status must travel with the content.
No-answer analysis is a product signal, not an edge case.

bodySections.title: Reframe the problem
bodySections.description: The real design question is not how to find similar text, but how to deliver the right approved context to the right role at the right node in the workflow.

bodySections.title: Editorial systems are architecture too
bodySections.description: Without clear review, ownership, and retirement patterns, even strong retrieval infrastructure turns into a trust problem for end users.

bodySections.title: Measure usefulness, not just relevance
bodySections.description: Useful retrieval reduces back-and-forth, supports action completion, and exposes moments the system cannot answer with confidence.

whyAgentExperienceNeedsJourneyDesign.title: Why agent experience needs journey design
whyAgentExperienceNeedsJourneyDesign.summary: Copilots fail when they are dropped in as a side widget instead of being designed around the task to be done.
keyPoints: Assistive AI should reduce decisions, not create another surface to manage.
Journey maps reveal where confidence and approval genuinely matter.
The best experiences blend automation with transparent next-best actions.

bodySections.title: From feature thinking to journey thinking
bodySections.description: Users do not want a chatbot feature; they want shorter, clearer completion paths with fewer risky decisions.

bodySections.title: Design for confidence
bodySections.description: Citations, source previews, and approval checkpoints make assistance easier to trust in real operating contexts.

bodySections.title: Design for handoff
bodySections.description: Human takeover, escalation, and case continuity should feel like a native flow, not a failure state.

Community.title: Connect practitioners, labs, and working groups
Community.summary: Join community programs around reusable AI delivery patterns, governance, and architecture practice.

buildersRoundtableQ3.title: Q3 builders roundtable
buildersRoundtableQ3.summary: A peer forum for platform, architecture, and product owners to align on reusable enterprise AI delivery patterns.

agenda.title: Reusable platform patterns
agenda.description: Share delivery accelerators, control models, and rollout sequencing across teams.

agenda.title: Assurance at scale
agenda.description: Compare evaluation practices, release gates, and business-facing evidence models.

agenda.title: Workshop
agenda.description: Map shared blockers and publish a short pattern pack after the event.

fieldAiPractitionerLab.title: Field AI practitioner lab
fieldAiPractitionerLab.summary: A practice program focused on frontline and field service AI patterns, from knowledge capture to edge delivery.
fieldAiPractitionerLab.audience: Operations leaders and field transformation teams

agenda.title: Workflow teardown
agenda.description: Break the current field journey into assist moments, control points, and measurable outcomes.

agenda.title: Architecture clinic
agenda.description: Review offline-first design choices, sync boundaries, and device telemetry needs.

agenda.title: Capability roadmap
agenda.description: Turn lab findings into a phased delivery roadmap with readiness checkpoints.

governedRagWorkingGroup.title: Governed RAG working group
governedRagWorkingGroup.summary: A recurring collaboration for teams building retrieval-dense experiences in regulated or high-trust domains.
governedRagWorkingGroup.audience: Knowledge platform teams and governance owners

agenda.title: Pattern review
agenda.description: Discuss content lifecycle, permission-aware retrieval, and three-lines-of-defense responsibilities.

agenda.title: Metrics exchange
agenda.description: Compare freshness, no-answer rates, and reviewer intervention across members.

agenda.title: Outcome sharing
agenda.description: Publish templates, governance checklists, and architecture reference notes for members.
